dialogos.texto_en: (After leaving American Village,\n we arrived at our next destination,\n the Okinawa Prefectural Museum.)
(I was honestly surprised\n that we almost caused a commotion...)
(Being quintuplets and all, dressed the same way,\n it can't be helped that they attract attention\n to some extent.)
Oh, I see...\nThis museum is different from what I imagined.\nIt's kinda stylish, or something.
This museum is combined with an art museum,\nso that might be related, don't you think?
Yeah, I can get that.
From this facility alone, you can learn about\nOkinawa's history, culture, and even its climate\nand art. That's a pretty good deal, right?
There's also a shop inside the museum.\nIt might be a good idea to buy some crafts\nor folk art as souvenirs.
Ah, they also have workshop events here.\nWhy don't we participate if we have time?
...Hey.\nAren't we already attracting attention\nfrom the people around us?
Ooh...\nWe're well aware of that too.
Us five are bound to stand out no matter what.
I mean, after all, you're five identical people.\nIt's only natural that you draw attention.
That's true...
What should we do?\nWe might end up in the same situation as before.
At American Village,\nwe started off doing things separately, didn't we?
Thanks to that,\nwe didn't attract any attention while sightseeing.
It seems like we should split up here too.
Yes, being separated\nwill definitely reduce the chance we're noticed,\ncompared to being all together.
Okay, let's freely move about for now,\nand everyone can explore as they like.
We'll meet up again at this entrance hall.
Okay.
Now...
(I'm pretty sure we came to this museum because Miku\n was interested in the relationship between Ryukyu\n history and Japanese military commanders.)
(If that's the case, there's a high probability\n that Miku will be the one who is most enthusiastic\n about the exhibits...right?)
(Should I start from there?\n Since I don't have any other clues at the moment?)
